commit.message:
Re-export Orientation from ars-core (#174)

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Hotfix sidebar description toggle and factual/prescriptive navigation (#3)

* Add survey analysis pipeline, sponsor-ready outputs and Streamlit cockpit

### Motivation
- Provide a sponsor-facing package to finalize the IA survey: clean data, produce charts, generate executive deliverables and an interactive Streamlit cockpit for decision-makers.
- Ensure deployability on Streamlit Cloud by declaring runtime dependencies and a UI theme for consistent presentation.
- Capture traceable artifacts (analysis report, one-pager, roadmap, governance, enablement plan, charts and cleaned CSV) for audit and review.

### Description
- Added a data analysis pipeline `analyze_survey.py` that loads the provided Excel survey, normalizes column names, produces `outputs/cleaned_data.csv`, generates charts under `outputs/charts/`, builds `outputs/variable_dictionary.csv` and writes multiple deliverables (`analysis_report.md`, `executive_summary.md`, `one_pager_comex.md`, `board_deck_outline.md`, etc.).
- Added a sponsor-facing Streamlit app in `app/streamlit_app.py` with `app/README.md`, using the generated `outputs` artifacts as sources and a custom theme in `.streamlit/config.toml` for consistent styling.
- Added runtime dependency management via `requirements.txt` including `streamlit`, `pandas`, `numpy`, `scipy` to ensure the app boots in Streamlit Cloud and local environments.
- Included the raw survey file and numerous `outputs/*.md` artifacts (diagnostics, delivery status, reviewer guide, roadmap, enablement plan, governance framework, demo script, etc.) to make the analysis and publication state explicit and reviewable.

### Testing
- Executed the analysis pipeline via `python analyze_survey.py`, which completed and printed a JSON status indicating `"status": "ok"` and `rows=70`, `raw_cols=91`, `clean_cols=33` (success).
- Compiled the Streamlit app with `python -m py_compile app/streamlit_app.py` with no syntax errors (OK).
- Launched Streamlit headless to validate boot with `timeout 20s streamlit run app/streamlit_app.py --server.headless true --server.port 8501`; startup was observed and stopped by the timeout, with no `ModuleNotFoundError` for `scipy` after adding `requirements.txt` (OK).
- Verified existence and sizes of key output artifacts and charts (`outputs/cleaned_data.csv`, `outputs/executive_summary.md`, `outputs/analysis_report.md`, `outputs/variable_dictionary.csv`, `outputs/charts/*.png`) as part of the final quality checks (OK).

* Implement phase 3 factual/recommendation navigation and artifacts

* Fix latest commit hash in delivery status

* Sync delivery status with current latest commit

* Make delivery status latest-commit field verification-based

* Hotfix sidebar Description seule navigation and status artifacts

* Update publication status after push/PR attempts

* Update hotfix publication status with PR #3 details

* fix(import): delegate to worker instead of inline processing in after()

Replace kickoffImportJob with startImportJob in all 4 import route
after() callbacks (finalize, multipart upload, commit, notify-customers).

startImportJob uses a clean strategy:
- Production with worker secret: delegates to /api/import-jobs/worker
  (runs in its own request context with maxDuration=300)
- Production without secret: throws to catch misconfiguration
- Non-production (localhost): falls back to inline processing

Previously, after() always ran processImportJobById inline first, which
claims the job then does heavy I/O. On Vercel, the function timeout
kills the process mid-work, orphaning the job with no recovery path.

Also adds logger.warn in triggerImportWorker when IMPORT_JOB_WORKER_SECRET
is missing as a defensive layer.

  only emitted when both filesystem AND keyboard categories are enabled

  where/winget) gated on hasFs; browser rules (open_url, open_browser_tab) gated
  on hasBrowser — prompt never advertises tools not in the active schema

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate 'Switched to' messages on provider list refresh

RefreshProvidersAsync sets _suppressProviderChange=true before calling
LoadProvidersAsync, but LoadProvidersAsync resets the flag to false before
returning. When RefreshProvidersAsync then sets ProviderComboBox.SelectedItem,
the SelectionChanged event fires unsuppressed and calls UpdateCurrentProvider
with showSwitchedMessage:true, showing a spurious 'Switched to X' message even
though the provider hasn't changed.

Fix: re-assert _suppressProviderChange=true immediately after LoadProvidersAsync
returns so the subsequent SelectedItem assignment is silent. The single explicit
UpdateCurrentProviderAsync(showSwitchedMessage:false) call at the end handles
any real provider state update.

commit.message:
feat(ci): 增加 latest 预编译发布通道 (#16)

## Why
- 需要让 `cargo binstall --git https://github.com/happy-proto/kat --force
kat` 优先安装 `master` 最新一次成功 CI 产出的预编译包。
- 当前 CI 只会上传 GitHub Actions artifact，还没有一个可持续覆盖更新的 GitHub Releases
channel。
- `latest` 这种 rolling channel 的资产命名、包内目录和仓库里的 `cargo-binstall`
元数据需要保持一致，否则安装链路会不稳定。

## What
- 扩展 release build matrix，补上 `x86_64-apple-darwin` 与
`x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`，并按 `cargo-binstall` 需要的固定结构打包各平台归档。
- 新增 `release latest` job：在 `master` 全绿后移动 `latest` tag，并覆盖更新同名 GitHub
prerelease 的资产。
- 在 `Cargo.toml` 中补 `repository` 与 `package.metadata.binstall`，让 `cargo
binstall --git` 可以直接解析 `latest` channel。
- 同步更新 `README.md`、`docs/maintenance.md` 和 `AGENTS.md`，记录 `latest`
channel 的安装方式与维护约定。

## Testing
- `cargo metadata --locked --no-deps`
- `uv run --with pyyaml python - <<'PY' ... yaml.safe_load(...) ... PY`
- `git diff --check`
- `cargo check --locked`

commit.message:
fix: loop detection warn action now sets X-Fairvisor-Warning header (#142)

When loop_detection.action is "warn", rule_engine._build_decision_from_loop
was checking loop_result.warning (a field never set by loop_detector.check)
to decide whether to emit the warning_code. This caused the X-Fairvisor-Warning
header to always be nil for the warn action.

Fix: use (loop_result.action == "warn") as the condition.

Adds a regression test: new scenario asserts that the warn action produces
an allow decision with X-Fairvisor-Warning: loop_warn header.

Fixes #141
